title: Software Engineer II
description:
Johns Hopkins University is seeking a Software Engineer II to join our IT team. You will design, develop, and maintain web applications supporting academic research and student services.

Responsibilities:
- Develop backend services using Python and Flask
- Build and maintain REST APIs consumed by frontend teams
- Write and optimize SQL queries and stored procedures in PostgreSQL
- Collaborate with cross-functional teams in an Agile/Scrum environment
- Contribute to CI/CD pipeline improvements using GitHub Actions and Docker

Requirements:
- 3-6 years of experience in software development
- Strong proficiency in Python and SQL
- Experience with REST API design
- Knowledge of Git and version control best practices
- Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science or related field

Preferred:
- Experience with Flask or Django
- Familiarity with Docker and containerization
- Experience working in a university or research environment

Salary: $80,000 - $105,000 annually

Johns Hopkins University is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of national origin. We welcome applications from all qualified individuals.
score_reasons: +4.0 skills match (python, flask, sql, postgresql, postgres)

title: Full Stack Developer
description:
The University of Michigan is hiring a Full Stack Developer to work with our Digital Innovation team. This role will develop web applications that support university research, administration, and student engagement.

Key Responsibilities:
- Develop full stack applications using JavaScript (Vue.js or React) on the frontend and Python/Flask or C#/ASP.NET on the backend
- Design and maintain SQL Server and PostgreSQL databases
- Implement and optimize T-SQL stored procedures
- Build RESTful APIs and integrate with third-party services
- Work in an Agile environment with sprint planning and daily standups

Minimum Qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or equivalent experience
- 4+ years of full stack development experience
- Proficiency in at least one JavaScript framework (Vue.js, React, or Angular)
- Strong SQL database skills
- Experience with Git and collaborative development

Desired:
- Experience with Docker and containerization
- Knowledge of CI/CD pipelines
- Healthcare or education sector experience a plus

Compensation: $85,000 - $110,000

The University of Michigan is a public research university and an equal opportunity employer committed to workplace diversity.

title: Data Engineer
description:
Mayo Clinic is seeking a Data Engineer to join our health data and analytics team. You will build and maintain data pipelines that power clinical research and patient care improvements.

Responsibilities:
- Design and implement ETL pipelines using Python
- Build and maintain data models in PostgreSQL and SQL Server
- Develop stored procedures and complex T-SQL queries
- Integrate data from electronic health records and clinical systems
- Support machine learning teams with data preparation

Requirements:
- 3-7 years of data engineering or software engineering experience
- Strong Python skills (pandas, SQLAlchemy, etc.)
- Expert-level SQL skills including T-SQL and stored procedures
- Experience with ETL design and data pipeline development
- Master's degree in Computer Science, Data Science, or related field preferred

Salary Range: $90,000 - $120,000

Mayo Clinic is a nonprofit academic medical center. We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment.
